title: Methodology for improving personal performance based on heart rate variability analysis
author: Alexander Balabanov
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: : This study proposes a comprehensive methodology for enhancing individual performance based on the analysis of heart rate variability - a key indicator of autonomic nervous system regulation and psychophysiological resilience. The approach focuses on the design of a dual-layer impact system that combines quantitative biometric data with qualitative psychological insights, allowing for a comprehensive understanding of how individuals adapt to stress. The method under consideration goes beyond standard heart rate variability (HRV) training by addressing both vagal modulation and cognitive restructuring, resulting in lasting improvements in emotional stability and executive function. The empirical study includes statistical comparisons between control and experimental groups, showing significant increases in HRV amplitude, coherence, and signal variability during the post-intervention period.
